title: Scalable Kernel-Based Distances for Statistical Inference and Integration
authors_affiliations: Masha Naslidnyk (Department of Computer Science, University College London; institution inferred from UCL thesis source)
conjecture_summary: Finite-sample consistency claim for empirical kernel quantile discrepancy eKQD_p, asserting expected error O(L^-1/2 + N^-1/2) for p>1 under bounded-density and kernel-moment assumptions.
verdict: Disconfirmed via a rank-1-kernel reduction to Gaussian empirical W_2, giving a slower sqrt(log log N / N) rate.
literature_overlap: Category (a) for the conjecture text itself: nearly exact in earlier KQE literature; the counterexample looks like a close new synthesis.
well_specifiedness: Not fully well-specified: gamma and gamma_L are undefined in isolation, and the projection randomness behind the L-term is omitted.

title: Steady-State Behavior of Constant-Stepsize Stochastic Approximation: Gaussian Approximation and Tail Bounds
authors_affiliations: Zedong Wang (H. Milton Stewart School of Industrial and Systems Engineering, Georgia Tech); Yuyang Wang (Department of Mathematics, University of Michigan); Ijay Narang (School of Computer Science, Georgia Tech); Felix Wang (H. Milton Stewart School of Industrial and Systems Engineering, Georgia Tech); Yuzhou Wang (School of Mathematics, Georgia Tech); Siva Theja Maguluri (H. Milton Stewart School of Industrial and Systems Engineering, Georgia Tech)
conjecture_summary: Uniform bounded-derivative solvability claim for a Stein equation associated with the paper's generalized SGD steady-state approximation.
verdict: Disconfirmed as written; the |y| test function breaks the claimed smooth bounded-derivative Stein solution at 0.
literature_overlap: No exact prior positive result because the statement is false; corrected nearby Stein-equation results are close in the literature (category (b)).
well_specifiedness: Poorly specified: notation is overloaded, Z is not defined locally, and nearby formulas for the target diffusion/operator are inconsistent.

title: On Graham's rearrangement conjecture
authors_affiliations: Huy Tuan Pham (Department of Mathematics, California Institute of Technology); Lisa Sauermann (Institute for Applied Mathematics, University of Bonn)
conjecture_summary: Graham rearrangement conjecture: every subset of Z_p \ {0} admits an ordering with pairwise distinct partial sums.
verdict: Unresolved; strong recent progress proves the conjecture for all sufficiently large primes but not all primes.
literature_overlap: Category (b): close versions exist, including all-sufficiently-large-prime results, but not the exact all-primes theorem.
well_specifiedness: Well specified with the immediately preceding definition of valid ordering; not fully self-contained as an isolated sentence.

title: Matroids from gain graphs over quotient groups
authors_affiliations: Zach Walsh (Department of Mathematics and Statistics, Auburn University)
conjecture_summary: Universal-model existence conjecture for the matroid class M(Gamma, Gamma_1, A) arising from Frobenius groups and their complements.
verdict: Unresolved; plausible universal-model candidates exist, but no proof or counterexample was found.
literature_overlap: Category (b): close universal-model results are known for the extreme special cases, but not this full interpolation theorem.
well_specifiedness: Reasonably well specified once the paper's definitions of M(Gamma, Gamma_1, A) and universal models are imported.

title: Continuity of Magnitude at Skew Finite Subsets of $\ell_1^N$
authors_affiliations: Sara Kalisnik (Pennsylvania State University); Davorin Lesnik (University of Ljubljana)
conjecture_summary: Asymptotic determinant conjecture for cubical thickenings of skew finite subsets of l1^N, predicting leading term 4^k r^k + O(r^{k+1}).
verdict: Disconfirmed by a one-dimensional counterexample F={0,1} in l1, where the determinant leading term is not 4^k r^k.
literature_overlap: False as stated; the counterexample is close to classical exponential-kernel determinant formulas (category (b)).
well_specifiedness: Reasonably well specified in-paper, but not fully self-contained as a standalone snippet because key notation is imported.

title: On the Hilbert depth of the quotient ring of the edge ideal of a complete bipartite graph
authors_affiliations: Andreea I. Bordianu (University Politehnica of Bucharest, Faculty of Applied Sciences); Mircea Cimpoeas (National University of Science and Technology Politehnica Bucharest, Faculty of Applied Sciences; Simion Stoilow Institute of Mathematics)
conjecture_summary: Four explicit product inequalities used to sharpen lower bounds on the Hilbert depth h(n,n) for edge ideals of complete bipartite graphs.
verdict: Confirmed; all four product inequalities hold, making the paper's conditional proposition unconditional.
literature_overlap: Not already proved verbatim, but very close to classical gamma-ratio/binomial-product inequalities (category (b)).
well_specifiedness: Mostly well specified; only a mild ambiguity remains about whether k is integer-only or any sufficiently large real value.

title: Optimal play in Yu-Gi-Oh! TCG is hard
authors_affiliations: Orazio Nicolosi (Department of Mathematics "Giuseppe Peano", University of Torino); Federico Pisciotta (Department of Mathematics, Computer Science and Physics, University of Udine); Lorenzo Bresolin (Department of Mathematics, University of Pisa)
conjecture_summary: Computability conjecture asserting that from a legal Yu-Gi-Oh TCG configuration and legal move one can compute the consequent configuration.
verdict: Disconfirmed under the paper's own later formalization: the next-state map is not single-valued on information states.
literature_overlap: The specific disconfirmation appears relatively novel, though the conjectural template is close to prior TCG/MTG complexity work.
well_specifiedness: Poorly specified: configuration semantics are informal and depend on hidden information, randomness, and evolving rules documents.

title: On the Classification of Perfect Prishchepov Groups
authors_affiliations: Layla Sorkatti (Department of Pure Mathematics, University of Khartoum and Al Neelain University); Ihechukwu Chinyere (Department of Mathematics and Applied Mathematics, University of Pretoria)
conjecture_summary: Classification conjecture saying a perfect Prishchepov group P(r,n,k,r-1,q) must be of type Z-tilde under the stated congruence exclusions.
verdict: Partially confirmed; the gcd(n,6)=1 case is proved, but the unrestricted statement remains open.
literature_overlap: Category (b): the unrestricted statement is not yet in the literature, but the coprime-to-6 case is now very close to complete.
well_specifiedness: Mathematically well specified in-paper, but not self-contained without the paper's group-theoretic definitions.

title: Quadratic irrational analogues of Ramanujan's series for $1/π$
authors_affiliations: John M. Campbell (Department of Mathematics and Statistics, Dalhousie University); Shaun Cooper (School of Mathematical and Computational Sciences, Massey University); Dongxi Ye (School of AI and Liberal Arts, Beijing Normal-Hong Kong Baptist University)
conjecture_summary: Question about the remaining N=15 irrational Ramanujan-type series, asking whether that lone case is already explained by the paper's transformation theory and known series network.
verdict: Not literally a yes/no conjecture, but under the intended reading the N=15 case is already settled positively by later results in the same paper.
literature_overlap: Category (a) if the target 2026 paper counts as literature: the intended resolution is already essentially present there; pre-2026 overlap is closer to category (b).
well_specifiedness: Poorly specified: the source text is a question, not a clean proposition, and leaves “related” undefined.

title: Quadratic irrational analogues of Ramanujan's series for $1/π$
authors_affiliations: John M. Campbell (Department of Mathematics and Statistics, Dalhousie University); Shaun Cooper (School of Mathematical and Computational Sciences, Massey University); Dongxi Ye (School of AI and Liberal Arts, Beijing Normal-Hong Kong Baptist University)
conjecture_summary: Cross-level modular-relation conjecture: the level-2 quadratic series with N in {25,45,85} should correspond to the level-10 series with N in {5,9,17}.
verdict: Confirmed under the paper's natural hypergeometric/modular notion of “related”, with pairings 25↔5, 45↔9, and 85↔17.
literature_overlap: Category (b): the exact draft wording is not stated verbatim in prior literature, but the proof mechanism is already very close to existing modular-transformation results.
well_specifiedness: Moderately specified once “related” is read in the paper's standard transformation-theoretic sense; otherwise still ambiguous.

title: Quadratic irrational analogues of Ramanujan's series for $1/π$
authors_affiliations: John M. Campbell (Department of Mathematics and Statistics, Dalhousie University); Shaun Cooper (School of Mathematical and Computational Sciences, Massey University); Dongxi Ye (School of AI and Liberal Arts, Beijing Normal-Hong Kong Baptist University)
conjecture_summary: Draft cross-level relation claiming that the series with N in {8,12,16,28} are related to the series with N in {2,3,4,7}.
verdict: Unresolved. The paper clearly handles the N=12 and N=28 subcases, but the full four-case statement remains too vague and the N=8 and N=16 cases are not proved there.
literature_overlap: Category (b): nearby transformation theory is already in the paper and earlier literature, but not the full exact four-case draft statement.
well_specifiedness: Poorly specified: “related” is undefined, the source comes from an old draft section, and the table references do not cleanly match the final PDF numbering.

title: Steady-State Behavior of Constant-Stepsize Stochastic Approximation: Gaussian Approximation and Tail Bounds
authors_affiliations: Zedong Wang; Yuyang Wang; Ijay Narang; Felix Wang; Yuzhou Wang; Siva Theja Maguluri
conjecture_summary: Uniform stationary-law existence and uniform 3h-moment bound for one-dimensional constant-stepsize SGD under Assumptions A5-A6.
verdict: Disconfirmed. For h=2 with quadratic f and heavy-tailed centered noise having finite third but infinite sixth moment, the stationary AR(1) law exists but has infinite sixth moment.
literature_overlap: Category (b): close constant-stepsize SGD stability theory exists under stronger dissipativity and higher-moment assumptions, but not the exact claim.
well_specifiedness: Not fully well specified: the centering and absolute-moment assumptions are unclear, and the stated hypotheses are too weak even in the quadratic test case.

title: Beyond singular value gaps in randomized subspace approximation
authors_affiliations: Christopher Wang; Alex Townsend
conjecture_summary: Improved randomized range-finder tail bound replacing the weaker constant by C_{N,k,p,δ} for general oversampling p.
verdict: Unresolved. The solver reduced special cases and recovered the proved p=0,1 and flat-spectrum regimes, but did not prove or refute the full p>1 statement.
literature_overlap: Category (b): very close gap-based and asymptotic canonical-angle bounds are known, and the same paper already proves nearby weaker and special-case variants.
well_specifiedness: Reasonably well specified in-paper but not self-contained without importing Theorem 3.1, the Frobenius singular value ratio ξ_k, and the constant C_{N,k,p,δ}.

title: Arrow pattern avoidance in permutations: structure and enumeration
authors_affiliations: Kassie Archer; Robert P. Laudone
conjecture_summary: Three explicit arrow-pattern enumeration formulas: 2^n-n, odd Fibonacci, and Motzkin.
verdict: Partially confirmed. The solver proved the odd-Fibonacci and Motzkin formulas, but did not settle the claimed 2^n-n formula.
literature_overlap: Category (b): the proven parts translate to standard noncrossing/nonnesting partition and Motzkin-path enumerations; the exact arrow phrasing appears newer.
well_specifiedness: Well specified in-paper once arrow-pattern avoidance and the fundamental bijection are imported.

title: On cyclically covering subspaces of $\mathbb{F}^n_q$
authors_affiliations: Yangcheng Li; Pingzhi Yuan; Shuang Li; Yuanpeng Zeng
conjecture_summary: Direct-sum max formula h_q(n)=max(h_σ(W_1),h_σ(W_2)) for σ-invariant summands of F_{q^n}.
verdict: Disconfirmed as written. Taking σ=id gives h_σ(...)=0 while h_2(3)=1; the likely intended Frobenius/shift version remains unsettled.
literature_overlap: Category (a) for the literal statement: the counterexample is immediate from the paper's own definitions. The repaired Frobenius-only version is closer to category (b).
well_specifiedness: Poorly specified: σ is only assumed to satisfy σ^n=id, which admits trivial operators not matching the earlier Frobenius identification.

title: Vigemers: on the number of $k$-mers sharing the same XOR-based minimizer
authors_affiliations: Florian Ingels; Antoine Limasset; Camille Marchet; Mikaël Salson
conjecture_summary: Complexity extension of the minimizer-counting dynamic program from XOR keys to arbitrary coordinatewise maps g_1,...,g_m.
verdict: Partially confirmed. The solver proved the claimed complexity for bijective coordinate maps but not for arbitrary noninjective maps, where the published recurrence scheme already breaks.
literature_overlap: Category (b): very close lexicographic and XOR-key counting results exist, but not the full arbitrary coordinatewise-function statement.
well_specifiedness: Not fully well specified: π_k^g(w) is displayed as a set rather than a count, and the paper blurs orders with preorders when the coordinate maps are noninjective.

title: Colored interlacing triangles and Genocchi medians
authors_affiliations: Natasha Blitvic; Leonid Petrov
conjecture_summary: Eventual integrality and leading coefficient 5^k for k! a_k(n), where a_k(n) is the q^k coefficient in the depth-2 colored-interlacing-triangle polynomial P_n(q).
verdict: Unresolved. The solver showed the integrality clause would follow formally from the preceding eventual-polynomiality conjecture, but did not prove the decisive leading-coefficient claim.
literature_overlap: Category (b)/(c): strong nearby Genocchi and Dellac literature exists, but not the exact fixed-k coefficient claim for this q-statistic.
well_specifiedness: Not fully self-contained; it only makes sense after importing the preceding eventual-polynomiality conjecture.

title: Algebraic representatives of the ratios $ζ(2n+1)/π^{2n}$ and $β(2n)/π^{2n-1}$
authors_affiliations: Luc Ramsès Talla Waffo
conjecture_summary: The smallest positive zero β_n of ~Ξ_n should tend to 0.
verdict: Confirmed after obvious typo repairs. The solver derived an exact coefficient-ratio identity giving 1/β_n ≳ -E_{2n+2}/E_{2n}, hence β_n→0.
literature_overlap: Category (a): a near-immediate follow-up paper studies the exact ~Ξ_n zero distribution; the proof route here appears different.
well_specifiedness: Nearly well specified but contains a typo (Λ_n for Ξ_n) and should restrict to n≥2, since ~Ξ_1 has no positive zero.

title: Polytopes of alternating sign matrices with dihedral-subgroup symmetry
authors_affiliations: Péter Madarasi
conjecture_summary: Dimension formula dim(P_QTSASM)=floor((n-1)^2/4)-2 for the quarter-turn symmetric ASM polytope.
verdict: Unresolved. The solver proved the conjectured quantity is an upper bound and checked n=5, but did not establish the matching lower bound or a counterexample.
literature_overlap: Category (b)/(c): close literature treats the relaxation and enumerates QTSASMs, but not this exact dimension formula.
well_specifiedness: Well specified in the paper's Section 6; the extracted plain-text snippet is garbled on its own.

title: New Types of Sturm bounds via $p$-adic transfer methods
authors_affiliations: William Craig
conjecture_summary: Optimal Sturm bound for level-1 quasimodular forms, including the full mod-m statement on the integral lattice ~M_{2k}^Z.
verdict: Partially confirmed. The solver proved the complex-coefficient uniqueness statement from the first d Fourier coefficients, but not the sharper mod-m claim for the full integral lattice.
literature_overlap: Category (b): close fixed-depth and depth-1 extremal/Sturm results exist, but not the exact full integral-lattice conjecture.
well_specifiedness: The complex half is clear; the mod-m half is under-specified unless one imports the paper's full integral lattice notation.
